raw_consumer_statement:
This complaint concerns the way Barclay handles disputes, which is inefficient and makes it extremely difficult for customers to receive proper service. After I opened a dispute and called twice to confirm that I was seeking a full refund, I received no communication indicating that the dispute was not resolved, but instead was closed. When reviewing my statements, I noticed that the charge I had disputed was reissued. According to their records, they reached out and left a voicemail, but I never received any communication.

I do not believe disputes should be closed until the party who opened them confirms that closure has been achieved. Disputes are opened for a reason, and a large bank should not simply close out dispute tickets to show completed cases.

I was then informed by the agent that she could not reopen my dispute over the phone and that I would need to send a fax or mail a letter to the Barclay Dispute Department. Why cant the agent reopen my case on the phone? Why is this process not as streamlined as accepting payments over the phone? A large bank is able to accept payments, ask for income and financial details, and perform other tasks over the phone, but when it comes to reopening a dispute to advocate for me the customer to get a refund for a service I can't get details for and want cancelled, the process becomes unnecessarily difficult.
